title: Oldenlandia rupicola
intro: Oldenlandia rupicola is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the "stone cliff oldenlandia" due to its unique habitat of growing on rocky cliffs and hillsides. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, thanks to its reported health benefits of antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The bioactive constituents of Oldenlandia rupicola include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Oldenlandia rupicola is often prepared into teas, infusions, or decoctions to treat conditions such as coughs, sore throats, and fever, and is also used to make capsules and tablets for oral consumption.
taxonomy: Oldenlandia rupicola belongs to the family Acanthaceae. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Oldenlandia. This genus is classified under the order Lamiales and the class Magnoliopsida. The family Acanthaceae is a large family of flowering plants commonly known as the acanthus family. Oldenlandia rupicola is a member of this family due to its shared characteristics and morphological features.
